inn-night.speaker: narrator
inn-night.text: The Lakeshore Inn at this hour is a different room. The lamps are down to embers. Tomas is asleep behind the counter, the way innkeepers sleep — half a mug from waking. She is at a corner table, hands wrapped around a wooden cup she has not drunk from. She watches you cross the room without standing.

mira-greet.speaker: mira
mira-greet.text: You came. I half hoped you would not. The other half is glad you did. That is the honest answer. I have been practising honest answers since the dock.

mira-pitch.speaker: mira
mira-pitch.text: I am not built for hauling rope or scrubbing pots. Tomas was kind about it. So — I made a different ask. If the work is honest enough that it does not need my name on a ledger, I would walk it with you. The hands stay quick. Quick hands have other uses.

mira-pitch-2.speaker: mira
mira-pitch-2.text: I will not lift from your purse. I will not lift from anyone you have called by name. Outside of that — assume I have done what I needed to do. Tell me what the work is.

choices.text: Walk with me. We start now.
Not tonight. I need to think.

recruit.speaker: mira
recruit.text: Aye. Then it is settled. I will pack what I have, which is not much. The cloak. A blade I should not be carrying. A small thing of my mother's, which is not yours to ask after yet.

recruit-done.speaker: mira
recruit-done.text: One rule, and it is mine. If we find a thing worth taking, I get the first look. Not the first pick. Just the look. A girl has to see the shape of a thing before she walks past it.

decline.speaker: mira
decline.text: Aye. Think, then. The hearth will be down to ash by the time you decide, but I have slept on colder boards. Find me here again when the answer has settled. I am not in a hurry. I have not been in a hurry for years.
